claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 6 years old and does not live with you.
  - Blake is 16 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child in local authority care for 9 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 16 and in approved education
  - Claimant contributes £30.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK; Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05); Child in local authority care for 9 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

First, could you please tell me how many children you would like to claim Child Benefit for, and what are their names and ages?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! I'd like to claim Child Benefit for my two children. I have Alex, who's 6 years old, and Blake, who's 16.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's great to know you have two children, Alex (6 years old) and Blake (16 years old).

Now, could you please confirm whether you currently live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't live in the UK. I'm currently living abroad.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. Since you're living abroad, there are some specific circumstances where you might still be eligible for Child Benefit.

Could you tell me a bit more about your situation? For example:

- Are you living in an EU/EEA country or Switzerland?
- Are you a Crown servant (such as a diplomat or member of the armed forces posted overseas)?
- Or is there another reason you're living abroad?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm just living abroad - I'm not a Crown servant or diplomat, and I'm not in one of those special work situations.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. Unfortunately, to qualify for Child Benefit, you generally need to be living in the UK.

There are some exceptions that allow people living abroad to claim, such as:
- Being a Crown servant posted overseas
- Working in certain countries under specific circumstances
- Having certain rights to reside

Since you've mentioned you're simply living abroad without any of these special circumstances, I need to ask one more question to be thorough:

Do you have any particular connection to the UK, such as:
- Having moved from the UK recently?
- Having settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme?
- Any other residency rights in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't have any of those connections. I've just been living abroad and don't have settled status or any special residency rights in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for confirming that. Based on the information you've provided, I now have enough details to make an assessment of your eligibility for Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, I'm sorry to say that **you are not eligible for Child Benefit** for either of your children.

Here's a summary of my assessment:

### Alex (aged 6) — **Not Eligible**
Although Alex meets the age requirement (being under 16), you do not meet the fundamental requirement of living in the UK. Since you are living abroad without any of the special circumstances that would allow overseas claims (such as being a Crown servant or having settled/pre-settled status), you cannot claim Child Benefit for Alex.

### Blake (aged 16) — **Not Eligible**
Similarly, even though Blake could potentially qualify based on age if they were in approved education or training, the same residency issue applies. Since you are living abroad without any qualifying circumstances, you cannot claim Child Benefit for Blake.

---

**What this means:** Child Benefit is generally only available to people who live in the UK. If your circumstances change in the future and you move to the UK (or gain the right to reside there), you may wish to revisit your eligibility at that time.

I'm sorry I couldn't give you better news. Is there anything else I can help you with?

reasonings: For Alex (aged 6): To qualify for Child Benefit, you normally need to live in the UK. While Alex is under 16 (which meets the age requirement), you have confirmed that you are living abroad, are not a Crown servant, do not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, and do not have any other special residency rights or connections to the UK. Therefore, you do not meet the residency requirement for Child Benefit for Alex.

For Blake (aged 16): To qualify for Child Benefit, you normally need to live in the UK. Although Blake is 16 and could potentially still qualify if they were in approved education or training, you have confirmed that you are living abroad, are not a Crown servant, do not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, and do not have any other special residency rights or connections to the UK. Therefore, you do not meet the residency requirement for Child Benefit for Blake.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for either child because they do not meet the fundamental UK residency requirement. They have confirmed they are living abroad without any of the special circumstances that would allow them to claim (such as being a Crown servant, having settled/pre-settled status, or having other residency rights in the UK). The UK residency requirement applies regardless of the children's ages or other circumstances.

Therefore, you do not meet the residency requirement for Child Benefit for Blake.

The UK residency requirement applies regardless of the children's ages or other circumstances.
